visualGoal: Make a DCS pilot want the right dual-lever, detented throttle for their airframe before they hit the setup-tax and single-airframe caveats. Real throttle/HOTAS product proof throughout; no procedural art. Two flagship throttles (Virpil MongoosT-50CM3, WinWing Orion 2) are held text-only until verified photos land.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Flagship throttle and the article's top overall pick, currently TEXT-ONLY in body because no verified image. Needs a real product photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or before certification. Path already referenced in product JSON but NOT in verified-product-images.json — so its gear page does not build and the internal /space/gear link was deliberately NOT used (Amazon-search link used instead).

Mid-tier stick-and-throttle value pick, TEXT-ONLY until a real photo is sourced. Confirm the grip variant (F-16EX vs F/A-18C) in the photo. No verified image -> no gear page -> body links to official store + Amazon search only.
Referenced only in passing as the platform piece for the Orion 2 ecosystem; low priority. Text-only.

Only the FULL HOTAS Warthog photo (thrustmaster-hotas-warthog.jpg) is verified in-house; there is no standalone dual-throttle-only verified image. Body honestly uses the full-HOTAS photo and notes the throttle also sells standalone at $349.99. A standalone dual-throttle photo would let the prestige gearpick show the throttle in isolation. Low priority — the full-HOTAS photo is an acceptable stand-in and IS verified.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dim DCS home-cockpit bench at night, cyan MFD glow and warm desk lamp, a rigid desk-clamp mount with an empty throttle mounting plate left open for a real dual-lever throttle product overlay, cable tray visible, curator Val Chen's hand resting near the afterburner-gate position on the empty mount, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake throttle, warped levers, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, invented cockpit UI

infographicPrompts.title: Anatomy of a DCS throttle detent
content: Afterburner detent set a hair below 100%
Dual levers split for asymmetric thrust
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, space/flight colors, real throttle photos as row markers for Codex overlay

content: 4 Stream Deck / MFD for bind overflow

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult space/flight-sim curator Val Chen in a clean dim home-cockpit bay, one hand on a dual-lever flight throttle mounted to a desk clamp, calm expert energy, product overlay space beside her, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: Optional: a DCS cockpit throttle-quadrant screenshot (e.g. F/A-18C or A-10C) showing the mil/afterburner gate, to illustrate the detent pro-tip. Use official DCS/ED media or first-party capture only; label as game media, do not present as product photo.
